commit.message:
fix: clamp last refresh to zero to prevent negative display (#138)

Prevents the -1 display by clamping elapsed time to zero, matching the
pattern already used in computeTickingLastSeen for last seen timestamps.

Fixes race condition where clockTickNow can be slightly behind
readerInfoReceivedAt when the tick fires.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: 付款里程碑列表实际回款日期查询不生效

前端发送 actualPaymentDateStart/End 作为顶级参数，Contract 域类没有对应字段无法自动绑定。
在 Controller 的 listWithContracts 和 sumPaymentAmount 中用 @RequestParam 单独接收，
手动放入 contract.getParams() 供 MyBatis XML 中 params.actualPaymentDateStart 使用。

commit.message:
停電（ブラックアウト）とライト復旧モジュールを追加

Firebase構造:
  blackout: { active: false, light: false }
  hostがactive、manualプレイヤーがlightを制御

JS:
- scheduleBlackout(): host側で10〜20秒後にactive=trueを書き込み、15秒後に自動復旧
- updateBlackoutOverlay(): active&&!lightの時にオーバーレイを表示
- setupFlashlightButton(): mousedown/touchstart→light=true、mouseup/touchend→light=false
- handleStateChange: phase2中のblackout変化をキー重複に関係なく毎回処理
- enterFinalArm/enterReview/backToTitle: blackoutHandleをクリア＆blackoutリセット

HTML:
- #blackout-overlay: bomb-p2のbomb-panel内に配置（z-index:60, position:absolute）
- #flashlight-btn: manual-p2に追加（長押し専用、PC/スマホ両対応）

CSS:
- .bomb-panel: position:relative; overflow:hidden 追加
- #blackout-overlay.active: 表示（flex+中央配置）
- #flashlight-btn.lit: 黄色グロー演出

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix skill suggestions: surface errors and fix name extraction

- Log and surface errors in fetchSkillSuggestions instead of silently
  swallowing them, making debugging easier
- Check data.error from backend before processing suggestions
- Add string name fallback in get_skill_suggestions to match search_skills
  extraction logic exactly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Queue action (Forge-s33) (#123)

* fix: make queue actions async so UI responds instantly

Queue action callbacks (Label/Close) were running synchronously in the
Bubbletea Update loop, blocking the UI for 3-5 seconds while the IPC
round-trip completed. Now they return tea.Cmd like the merge action
pattern — the menu closes immediately and results arrive via
QueueActionResultMsg.

Bead: Forge-s33

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review comments for Forge-s33

Return the tea.Cmd from executeQueueAction in the 'l' key handler
so the async IPC call actually executes and delivers results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eagerly initialize lazy collections in findAllWithRoutes (OSIV disabled)

With spring.jpa.open-in-view=false the Hibernate session closes before
Jackson serializes the response, causing LazyInitializationException on
Route.routeStops and Route.trips. Use Hibernate.initialize() within the
@Transactional boundary to force-load them. @BatchSize(20/10) already
on the fields ensures batch queries instead of N+1.

commit.message:
Scientific integrity pass: honest validation documentation and code fixes

All 5 critical audit findings addressed:

1. Module docstring: remove "peer review scrutiny" and "empirically validated" overclaims;
   replace with precise sigma formula, validation status, and calibration disclaimers.

2. get_validation_report(): add methodology_note (N=4, LOOCV, circular), Wilson 95% CIs
   on sensitivity/specificity/F1, sigma_semantics block (formula + assumptions + caveats),
   bayesian_calibration block (prior/LR sources, ceiling, no Brier score), minimum_n note.

3. Cross-validation log: "F1=1.000" → "Internal consistency check (N=4, LOOCV) — NOT
   a generalisation estimate" with explicit warning.

4. Analysis metadata: replace "Bayesian evidence fusion with cross-validation" with
   accurate description noting hardcoded LRs and uncalibrated priors.

5. docs/scientific/VALIDATION_INTEGRITY.md (NEW): comprehensive audit document covering
   all 5 critical checks, exact code locations, confirmed issues, and a three-phase
   remediation roadmap. Summary table marks each point as CONFIRMED/SPURIOUS/MISSING.

6. docs/engineering/sigma5_success_criteria.md: add critical caveat under Phase 3
   verification log; add precise sigma formula definition with bonus and correlation
   disclaimers; correct F1=1.000 attribution.

Tests: 90 pass / 0 fail (unchanged).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

save supervisor context snapshot at cycle #150: P5b done, 4 diagnostic experiments running

commit.message:
Move themeColor from metadata to viewport export

Next.js 15 requires themeColor in a separate viewport export.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: handle AI returning JSON array for multi-page topology

Claude sometimes returns a top-level JSON array [ {...}, {...} ] instead
of a single object when generating multi-page SLDs. The previous parser
only matched {…} via regex, breaking on array responses with "Failed to
parse topology JSON" error.

- Try JSON.parse on the full stripped string first (handles both arrays and objects)
- If direct parse fails, try extracting array [...] or object {...} via regex
- If parsed result is an array, merge all page topologies into one (feeders concatenated)
- Applied fix in sld-generation.service.ts and both controller endpoints

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
DRAM-sharded GEMV: 12 optimal cores, bulk reads, TRID pipelining (~247ms/tok)

Replace 56-core interleaved GEMV with 12-core DRAM-sharded dispatch:
- Each core reads from exactly one DRAM bank (zero NOC contention)
- Weight buffers sharded via BufferDistributionSpec across 12 banks
- Bulk contiguous noc_async_read per block instead of per-tile reads
- TRID pipelining (alternating TRIDs 1/2) overlaps reads with compute
- BLOCK=16 empirically optimal (tested 4, 16, 64, 128)
- ~32% improvement: 363ms/tok → 247ms/tok

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add IPICO reader control protocol implementation (#137)

## Summary
Implements full IPICO reader TCP control protocol (port 10000) with
bidirectional socket handling, request/response matching, and real-time
SSE events. Adds reader info polling (statistics, extended status,
date/time), clock synchronization, read mode control, and record
clearing. Includes comprehensive protocol documentation
reverse-engineered from pcap captures.

## Changes
- **ipico-core**: Control module with BCD encoding, LRC checksums,
command encoding, and response parsing (724 lines, 32 tests)
- **forwarder**: Reader control client, connection sequence, 30s status
polling, 6 HTTP API endpoints, SSE event broadcasts
- **forwarder-ui**: Reader info display, clock drift formatting,
expandable per-reader detail panel with 4 control buttons (Sync Clock,
Read Mode, Refresh, Clear Records)
- **docs**: Protocol specification with frame formats, encoding rules,
state diagrams, and 5 pcap captures demonstrating control sequences

## Testing
- cargo test -p ipico-core: 21 passed
- cargo test -p forwarder: all suites passed
- npm run check: 0 errors/warnings  
- npm run test: 26/26 passed
- cargo build --workspace: clean
- cargo build -p forwarder --features embed-ui: clean

## Notes
- Handles concurrent bidirectional control on shared reader TCP socket
via tokio::io::split
- Request/response matching with 2s timeout per command
- 3-step clear records sequence with CONFIG3 cycling per protocol
specification
- Clock drift calculation vs local time for UI display

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Infura budget: recalibrate for 3M/day (not month) — remove throttling

Infura free tier = 3M credits/DAY = 125k/hr. Our max projected usage
(all 5 chains at full block rate) is ~2,820 calls/hr = 2.3% of budget.

- HOURLY_CAP: 3,000 → 100,000 (safety net, not a throttle)
- CHAIN_MIN_INTERVAL: 30s → 2s (just blocks duplicate events, not real limiting)
- Arbitrum/Avalanche poll interval: back to 15s (was 30s due to old min interval)

All chains now run at natural block frequency. Override via env vars if needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: evidence compression, LangSmith tracing, remove degraded mode

(a) Evidence compression — prevents LLM context-window overflow:
- Add 6 pure compression functions to rca_agent.py:
  count_tokens, compress_infra_findings, compress_metrics,
  compress_logs, compress_dag, compress_trace_deviations
- compress_infra_findings: priority-ordered (critical_events first,
  node_health dropped first); zero-restart pods filtered out
- compress_metrics: collapses raw Prometheus vector format to
  {NF: {metric: value}} summary dicts; drops NFs if over budget
- compress_logs: scores entries by DAG-match > ERROR > WARN > rest;
  truncates long messages; stops at token budget
- compress_dag: cascading strip (keywords/success_log → fp-only
  phases → truncate) keeping first+last phases
- compress_trace_deviations: slices to rca_max_deviations_per_dag,
  drops empty DAGs, then drops lowest-deviation DAGs
- compress_evidence orchestrates all 5 compressors and returns
  prompt-template-ready strings
- rca_agent_first_attempt now calls compress_evidence instead of
  inline format_* calls; total evidence targets ~3500 tokens
- Add 7 token-budget config fields (rca_token_budget_*) and 2
  supporting fields (rca_log_max_message_chars, rca_max_deviations_per_dag)
- Add ~20 unit tests across 7 new test classes

(b) LangSmith tracing — auto-wires env vars on startup:
- Add langchain_tracing_v2, langchain_project, langchain_endpoint
  config fields with @model_validator that calls os.environ.setdefault
  when tracing is enabled (preserves pre-existing env vars)
- LANGSMITH_API_KEY injected only when langsmith_api_key is set
- .env documents LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=false as default
- 4 new tests in TestLangSmithConfig

(c) Remove degraded mode fallback:
- Delete degraded_mode_analysis() from rca_agent.py
- Remove try/except around llm_analyze_evidence; LLM errors now
  propagate cleanly to LangGraph (triage marked failed, not silently
  producing low-confidence rule-based output)
- Remove degraded_mode and degraded_reason from TriageState,
  config.py (4 degraded_conf_* fields), graph.py, conftest.py,
  generate_final_report, and rca_agent return dict
- Replace TestLLMTimeoutHandling with TestLLMErrorPropagation
  (2 tests using pytest.raises)
- Remove degraded_mode assertions from test_state, test_graph,
  test_dag_mapper, test_webhook_e2e, verify_metrics_live

Also: add missing import logging + logger to rca_agent.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Initial implementation: ModSync Forge 1.20.1 mod

- Server: ModpackManager scans clientmodpack/ folder, builds MD5 manifest
- Server: Embedded HTTP server (port 8765) serves manifest JSON and mod JARs
- Server: Sends ModManifestPacket to clients on login
- Network: Forge SimpleChannel with ModManifestPacket (encode/decode)
- Client: ModComparator diffs local mods/ vs server manifest
- Client: ModDownloader fetches missing/outdated mods via HTTP
- Client: SyncScreen with progress bars and Restart button
- Client: Disables extra mods by renaming to .jar.disabled
- Config: HTTP port and enabled flag via Forge config
